threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the bus stop instead of just checking your phone. A classic rookie mistake.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the entire bus with his guitar. Surprisingly decent, but the bus driver seemed more embarrassed than us.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a street musician playing Bach on a saxophone while a group of kids were breakdancing nearby. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble upon a club inside a converted public toilet. The graffiti art alone is worth the trip.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively know to avoid Civic Center during rush hour, especially with BART in chaos. It's a wild ride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo juggling bags of takeout on the Muni yesterday while giving a TED talk to an invisible audience. Only in 'Frisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; they miss the quiet charms of our local plazas, where real life unfolds without the snap of a camera.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still say 'Adéu' instead of 'Adiós' when leaving a bar—locals switched to the Spanish version years ago.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Seeing someone pull out a paper map during the tube strike is a clear sign. Locals just know the way around the chaos.
Caught the 59 last night, and a guy was trying to sell a parrot he'd trained to shout 'Brixton!' every time we hit a speed bump.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It was the sound of a saxophonist jamming outside the Spätkauf at 2 AM, while a busker's cat lounged on the pavement, completely unfazed.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Saturday market where a DJ spins vinyl while you sip local craft beer and munch on Turkish street food. That's Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the exact bus line that gets delayed due to a dog on the tracks—like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo waiting on the Muni, clutching a bouquet—his bride was stuck on the train ahead. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all local bars serve tapas but many still just do cafe amb llet. You'll miss some real charm if you only seek out the trendy spots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot a recent arrival by the way they still believe they can get through Gràcia's labyrinthine streets without Google Maps.
